pr.title: Sort emails newest-first by default with sortable email list and polished card UI
pr.body:
Email list had no sort order and plain divider-separated rows with no visual hierarchy for unread state or sender identity.

## Sorting
- `PSTRepository.loadMessages()` now sorts by `getMessageDeliveryTime()` descending (nulls last) as the default
- `EmailListActivity` gains an overflow sort menu with four options: **Newest first** (default), **Oldest first**, **Sender A–Z**, **Subject A–Z**
- Sort and search compose: re-sorting respects the active query filter via a unified `applyFilterAndSort()` path

## Email list item redesign
- Each row is now a `MaterialCardView` (8dp horizontal margin, 4dp vertical margin, 1dp elevation, 8dp radius)
- **Unread indicator**: 4dp blue left stripe, visible only on unread messages (replaces alpha/typeface-only approach)
- **Sender avatar**: 40×40dp oval with sender's first initial (`bg_avatar.xml`), replacing the blank left edge
- Unread emails bold both sender and subject; read emails use normal weight throughout

```xml
<com.google.android.material.card.MaterialCardView
    app:cardCornerRadius="8dp"
    app:cardElevation="1dp" ...>
  <View android:id="@+id/viewUnreadIndicator"
        android:layout_width="4dp"
        android:background="@color/primary"
        android:visibility="gone" />
  <TextView android:id="@+id/tvAvatar"
            android:background="@drawable/bg_avatar" ... />
  <!-- sender / subject / preview / attachment rows -->
</com.google.android.material.card.MaterialCardView>
```

- Removed `DividerItemDecoration`; card margins provide visual separation
pr.author: Copilot
